title: Auranticarpa resinosa
intro: Auranticarpa resinosa, also known as the blue beech, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Australia. The resin of Auranticarpa resinosa has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, coughs, and sore throats, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include aurantionols, resinols, and limonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Auranticarpa resinosa is commonly used in traditional herbal medicine, where it is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat respiratory and inflammatory conditions.
